---
title: "Week 2: NYT API"
subtitle: "EDS 231 — Spring 2026"
date: 
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    highlight: pygments
    css: week-styles.css
    toc: false
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: true
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

> An introduction to text preprocessing and the New York Times article API.

---

### Learning Objectives

By the end of this week, you will be able to:

- Make API calls in R — constructing URLs with query parameters and authenticating with an API key
- Conduct exploratory data analysis — visualizing temporal patterns in media coverage using ggplot2
- Apply string manipulation — cleaning strings using functions like str* and gsub()
- Use stop word dictionaries — removing common uninformative words from text data to prepare it for analysis

---

### Recommended Reading

📖 [TMR 1.0-1.3](https://www.tidytextmining.com/tidytext.html),
   [SMLTR Appendix A](https://smltar.com/regexp.html#regexp)

---

## Lecture Materials

### Slides

🖥 Slide deck from lecture

[View Slides](https://ucsb.box.com/s/e4bjyt0nhzjgb01wt1ekd2ts3gt3uo6o) 

### Code & Data

💻 R scripts used during class:

[Lab 1 In-class.Rmd](https://github.com/MaRo406/EDS-231-text-sentiment/blob/main/Labs/Lab1.Rmd)

[key-Lab1.Rmd](https://github.com/MaRo406/EDS-231-text-sentiment/blob/084b78fa0dec0dd41b686b014bc04edd1609c0a4/Labs/key-Lab1.Rmd)

---

## 📂 Assignments

### Lab 1 — NYT API <span class="badge badge-danger">Graded · 11 pts</span>


**Due: April 14 @ 11:59 pm**

Complete the assignment laid out in Lab1. Submit your .Rmd and knitted document (HTML or PDF) to Canvas.

---
